name: HOTEL YAH HANOI & SPA
detail: The best part is the bathroom, ultra clean

name: Little Charm Hanoi Hostel
detail: I was so lucky to have found Little Charm Hanoi Hostel. I almost booked a different hostel, until I stumbled upon this on Agoda! It's one of the best hostels I've stayed at in my 7 months of continuous traveling. The first hostel where beds are made for you daily and towels can be changed daily if you wish. Lots of toilets and shower rooms on every floor so you won't have to queue. Everyone in the staff was GENUINELY NICE. Zoe on the front desk was always helpful and Antonio the Spanish-speaking vietnamese staff. Guy who checked me in was so nice as well, but I didn't get his name. Breakfast was amaaaazing. I did not expect it to be so good. You are given lots of options on a menu card, but on top of that, there's even a buffet spread for fruits, vegetables and drinks (juices, vietnamese coffee, tea, water). Not only that, the hostel even has a pool with jets! The common area is also nice. You get a quiet work area on the second floor, or you can hang out at the restaurant as all their seats are comfy and all areas are work-conducive. So handy when you're working while traveling like me. It was so good, we extended 2 more nights (direct at the hostel) and we booked again for when we come back to Hanoi. Also, the hostel holds your luggages for you (in a storage room with bag tags) if you want to check out and go to Sapa/Halong Bay for a couple of days. Really can't ask for more! If you don't stay here when you're in Hanoi, you'd be missing out on a lot! Hanoi is chaotic but this hostel is your little oasis in the middle of all that! The best!

name: Azumaya Kim Ma 3
detail: Great location, Japanese style breakfast is also good

name: Hanoi Railway Capsule
detail: I felt safe and all the staff are helpful and accommodating.

name: Luxury Backpackers
detail: Hadn’t used Agoda since in Australia, thought I’d give it a punt after a very hot and busy room in the VBH two doors down. On offer for $6 AUD a night which is a fantastic deal. Stayed in the ten bed mixed, each bunk has its own privacy curtain, with power points and reading light next to the bed. The room is constantly air conditioned, with two fans to circulate it round the room - a great addition after the 40c Hanoi heat. My mate who is 6ft 4 had no issue with the length of his bed. WiFi available in the dorm rooms, a little intermittent at times but nothing that caused an issue. As with many places in Vietnam, shower and toilet in the same room but not a problem as very clean and good locks. 2 “wet rooms” in the 10 bed share. We’d already booked on to a Ha Long Bay tour, enjoyed it so much came straight back after the tour. Bar (fridge) is reasonably priced, big Dasani water @10k, Hanoi bottled beer @30k. Free breakfast, as with other hostels in Vietnam, some form of eggs and a baguette. (Bacon/sausage available for meat eaters) Tea, Coffee and fresh fruit on offer as well as cooked. Vietnamese breakfast option available too but didn’t have it. Reasonably priced food available on full menu throughout the day too. 10 minute walk from beer corner. Cannot recommend this hostel enough, good on you Luxury Backpacker Hanoi

name: Melia Hanoi Hotel
detail: There are many hotels listed in Hanoi. If you are on a limited budget or a backpacker, you can choose from hundreds in the old quarter. That's fine if you like no windows or noisy environments. Local knowledge is invaluable and a friend in Hanoi said there was only one area to stay and that is in the embassy areas. Melia offered a great deal on Agoda for a deluxe room. It's in a great area in the city so you can walk to the Opera and museums and there are a wealth of western-style eateries, coffee shops with free wifi etc. So, location is fabulous. Check-in was efficient and fast. My deluxe room was finished well but an old tv and no DVD spoiled it somewhat. At this price point, a plasma and DVD should be considered mandatory. WiFi/internet is supplied for a price. Would a hotel provide a room without a TV nowadays? Of course not, so why this silly idea that internet is not required? Why do many hotels still think it is ok to charge for an essential service? Let me say it loudly so that hotel managements hear this: we want internet in our rooms and elsewhere in the hotel and it should be provided for free at a five star hotel. The Melia is everything a five star establishment should be: courteous, efficient staff, lovely spacious reception area/lobby, fast lifts (elevators to our US friends), lovely pool at a gorgeous temperature (but silly towel policy whereby you can arrive to the pool area and find no towels and have to go back inside to the health club and sign for one (!!!). This is just a silly policy that needs changing. Towels should be available at the pool where you use them. The health club was very impressive with lots of programmes offered for those who go for that sort of thing. Breakfast offered a huge selection (my booking included breakfast for two)
area: Hoan Kiem District,Hanoi - View on map

name: The Lapis Hotel
detail: I love every details of this Lapis hotel in Hanoi . As that was My last stop in Vietnam trip before finishing off my holiday in where I didn’t expect too much in Hanoi initially. Very pleasant ending trip . Hardware : rooftop swimming pool , Mosaic of the Jacuzzi , even the small thing like the light switch / electric button / toilet / bath room & toiletries etc........( compared with other hotels i stayed in Vietnam such as SGN DAD ...) Software: All staffs are friendly , smiley & very willing to help , very well communicate skill, special thanks to Nguyen Hong Thai the manager & Mai, Hong the reception all staffs were taking extra care of my needs ...as to compensate my loss of 1st night stay in the Lapis hotel ( By no mean , I had bad experience in others hotel where I were moved in with unexpected situation ... Mmmm I totally felt so unlucky for my 1st time / 1st night in Hanoi - having said that I only liked the balcony in that room for that hotel to be fair x but nothing else I liked there ) sorry , have to be very honest about my feelings . Highlights : I would say the 2nd night in Lapis hotel wiped off the upsetting feelings... but I still would love to stay there longer than 1 night though 😆 seems like ordered full set meal but missing my (main) dessert at the end... I would have given full points (100) for my stay if I could have stayed the total 2 nights in the Lapis hotel as booked initially .”) Now is 95 points. Definitely will come back & stay in this hotel again , please keep it up ;)
